key_drivers: The dollar stayed firm into the U.S. close: TradingView showed DXY at 100.193, up 0.33% on March 27.
Volatility stayed decisively defensive: Cboe listed VIX spot at 31.05, up 13.16%, while FedWatch still showed 97.9% odds of no change at the next meeting.
Official U.S. macro rates moved against duration: DFII10 rose to 2.08 from 2.02 and HY OAS widened to 3.21 from 3.17, while GDPNow held at 2.0% rather than reaccelerating.
Semiconductor leadership remained weak even as rate volatility eased intraday: SOX fell 1.69% to 7,457.67, while MOVE fell 2.67% to 111.9456.
Several cross-asset inputs remained carried forward from the earlier repository factor snapshot, including oil inventories, gold ETF flows, some central-bank differentials, and China-growth proxies.

stale_sources: Gold ETF flows (not directly re-verified in this run)
EIA crude inventories and oil supply classification (carried forward from prior repository factor snapshot)
China-growth proxy and some FX rate-differential inputs (carried forward from prior repository factor snapshot)
ECB, RBA, and BoJ stance inputs were not fully re-documented from fresh official statement pages in the final notes of this run
fallbacks_used: TradingView for DXY, SOX, and MOVE live direction checks
Cboe VIX page for spot volatility level and daily move
Atlanta Fed GDPNow page for current Q1 growth nowcast
FRED latest official prints for DFII10, HY OAS, and T10Y2Y
Repository factor snapshot for stale carried-forward oil, ETF-flow, and some FX-specific inputs
overnight_changes: Real yields rose 6 bps day over day, increasing pressure on duration-sensitive assets.
Credit spreads widened while the 2s10s curve steepened, leaving U.S. cyclicals mixed rather than uniformly weak.
The combination of stronger USD and a VIX above 30 kept the broader cross-asset regime defensive through the session.

imminent: Iran / Strait of Hormuz headlines affecting oil and risk sentiment
background: Next ECB / RBA / BoJ communication for FX-differential repricing
